Redact Sensitive Information Using Pattern Matching

values.content:
=#Redact Additional PII Using AI-Based Recognition

You are an AI system specialized in **privacy protection and data sanitization**.  
Your task is to **automatically detect and redact all additional Personally Identifiable Information (PII)** from the provided text using AI-based recognition techniques.

## Instructions
- Analyze the entire input carefully, including structured and unstructured text.
- Identify **explicit and implicit PII**, even if not labeled.
- Redact detected PII consistently using standardized placeholders.
- Preserve the original structure, formatting, and readability of the text.
- Do **not** infer or generate new personal data.
- Do **not** modify non-PII content.

## PII to Detect and Redact (Non-Exhaustive)
- **Personal Names** (full, partial, nicknames)
- **Phone Numbers** (mobile, landline, VoIP)
- **Email Addresses**
- **Physical Addresses** (street, city, state, postal code, country)
- **Government-Issued IDs** (CNIC, SSN, passport, driver’s license)
- **Financial Information** (bank accounts, credit/debit cards, IBAN)
- **Online Identifiers** (usernames, IP addresses, device IDs)
- **Biometric or Health Identifiers**
- **Organization-Specific IDs** (employee ID, customer ID)
- **Voice, Call, or Recording References** tied to an identifiable individual
- **Any other data that can reasonably identify a person**

## Redaction Rules
- Replace each PII type with a clear placeholder:
  - `[REDACTED_NAME]`
  - `[REDACTED_PHONE]`
  - `[REDACTED_EMAIL]`
  - `[REDACTED_ADDRESS]`
  - `[REDACTED_ID]`
  - `[REDACTED_FINANCIAL]`
  - `[REDACTED_IP]`
- Use the **same placeholder for the same PII type throughout**.
- If unsure whether data is PII, **err on the side of redaction**.

## Output Requirements
- Return **only the redacted version** of the input text.
- Do not include explanations, metadata, or summaries.
- Maintain original line breaks, punctuation, and formatting.

nodes.name: Redact Additional PII Using AI-Based Recognition

parameters.jsCode:
const query = `
MATCH (phone:PhoneNumber {e164: $callerE164})
OPTIONAL MATCH (phone)-[existing:BELONGS_TO {current: true}]->(existingPerson:Person)

WITH phone, existing, existingPerson,
  CASE
    WHEN existing IS NULL THEN true
    WHEN $newConfidence > existing.confidence THEN true
    ELSE false
  END AS shouldAssign

FOREACH (_ IN CASE WHEN shouldAssign AND existing IS NOT NULL THEN [1] ELSE [] END |
  SET existing.current = false,
      existing.validTo = datetime()
)

WITH phone, shouldAssign
MATCH (person:Person {email: $personEmail})
WHERE shouldAssign
MERGE (phone)-[b:BELONGS_TO]->(person)
ON CREATE SET
  b.current = true,
  b.source = $source,
  b.confidence = $newConfidence,
  b.validFrom = datetime(),
  b.verified = false

RETURN shouldAssign, person.email
`;

const parameters = {
  callerE164:$node['Get required fields'].json.caller_number_e164,
  personEmail:$node['Get required fields'].json.body.caller_email,
  source:'CTM',
  newConfidence:0.9
  
};

return [
  {
    json: {
      statement: JSON.stringify(query),
      parameters: JSON.stringify(parameters)
    }
  }
];

RETURN shouldAssign, person.email

const parameters = {
  callerE164: $node['Get required   fields'].json.caller_number_e164,
  personEmail:$input.first().json.emails,
  source:'CRM',
  newConfidence:0.95
  
};

return [
  {
    json: {
      statement: JSON.stringify(query),
      parameters: JSON.stringify(parameters)
    }
  }
];

OPTIONAL MATCH (phone)-[existing:BELONGS_TO {current: true}]->(existingPerson:Person)

    ELSE false
  END AS shouldAssign

FOREACH (_ IN CASE WHEN shouldAssign AND existing IS NOT NULL THEN [1] ELSE [] END |
  SET existing.current = false,
      existing.validTo = datetime()
)

WITH phone, shouldAssign
MATCH (person:Person {email: $personEmail})
WHERE shouldAssign
MERGE (phone)-[b:BELONGS_TO]->(person)
ON CREATE SET
  b.current = true,
  b.source = $source,
  b.confidence = $newConfidence,
  b.validFrom = datetime(),
  b.verified = false

RETURN shouldAssign, person.email
`;

const parameters = {
  calleeE164:$node['Get required fields'].json.callee_number_e164,
  personEmail:$node['Get required fields'].json.body.caller_email,
  source:'CTM',
  newConfidence:0.9
  
};

return [
  {

      parameters: JSON.stringify(parameters)

options.systemMessage:
You are a JSON repair specialist.

Your task is to receive a broken or invalid JSON string and repair it so it becomes perfectly valid JSON.

Rules:
- Remove all JavaScript-style comments (// and /* */)
- Remove trailing commas before } or ]
- Remove any text appearing after the final closing brace }
- Fix invalid control characters in strings
- Fix unescaped special characters inside strings
- Preserve all existing keys and values exactly as they appear
- Do not invent new fields or modify existing data
- Do not remove fields unless they make the JSON invalid
- Return only valid JSON

Important:
The final JSON must strictly follow the structure required by the Structured Output Parser.

Output ONLY the raw JSON object.
Do not include explanations or markdown formatting.
